description: Taken as a whole, a tree or village is 'whalo' when its size is large for the comparison at hand. The word also works for amounts and changes with broad scope. When one dimension matters, 'losha' (wide) and 'laeno' (long) say which one.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiceless 'wh', then open into central 'a'. The tongue tip meets the alveolar ridge for 'l' before rounded mid-back 'o'. Stress falls on 'wha'.
sound_symbolism: The breathy opening and open 'a' give the first syllable room. The lateral movement into rounded 'o' lets it taper rather than stop; heard as a whole, the contour feels broad.
usage_notes: No unit or fixed threshold comes with 'whalo'. A village may be large beside nearby settlements and small beside a city.

semantic_domains.physical: overall size or amount considered against a contextual scale
semantic_domains.spatial: considerable extent without selecting a particular dimension
